metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists across any source. The Salesforce opportunity Description field is empty. No Avoma calls were discovered (zero matched buyer calls via opp+account discovery). Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, and Compelling_Event__c fields are all null. There is no articulated pain, no savings target, no cycle-time reduction, no headcount efficiency, and no ROI discussion on record. For a 150K S2P transformation targeting a large CPG, the absence of any quantified value case after 181 days signals the deal has not progressed beyond a preliminary inquiry. The immediate move is to run a discovery call with the procurement or finance lead to uncover the business problem, quantify the pain, and anchor the opportunity to a measurable outcome.

champion.narrative: No champion exists. X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c is null, Champion_Identified__c is null, and zero contact roles (SF contact roles query returned zero rows) mean no internal advocate is mapped. Zero Avoma calls mean no buyer has demonstrated advocacy (introductions, sharing internal docs, pushing back on competitors, driving the agenda). The only named individual in the evidence is Carlos Javier Pena Castellanos, who appeared in a prior sweep's stakeholder map but has zero source attribution in current Salesforce data (no contact role, no Avoma attendance, no task/event association). For a 150K S2P transformation at a large CPG after 181 days, the absence of any champion means there is no one inside Grupo Nutresa navigating the buying committee, providing competitive intelligence, or driving urgency. The deal is politically exposed. The move is to identify and develop a champion: ask the existing contact (if any) who internally is frustrated with the current S2P stack, who has the most to gain from a modern procurement platform, and who has credibility with the CFO/CPO. Then invest in building that relationship (exclusive insights, exec access, co-creation of the business case).

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the confirmed competitor. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is populated with 'SAP Ariba' (authoritative). SAP Ariba is the market leader in S2P and is likely the incumbent or the default option for a large CPG. Grupo Nutresa may already be using Ariba (or evaluating it as the safe choice given SAP's ERP footprint in Latin America). The wedge against Ariba is Merlin (agentic AI for negotiation, intake, and sourcing) plus the depth of the Zycus S2P suite (iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRequest, iLogix, iRisk, ANA), which Ariba does not match in breadth or AI maturity. Ariba is strong in network effects (supplier connectivity) and SAP integration, but is expensive, complex to configure, and lacks the agentic AI capabilities that Merlin delivers (autonomous negotiation, intelligent intake, predictive sourcing). The activation is to position Merlin as the Phase 2 centerpiece in the RFP response, run a live demo of the Merlin negotiation agent on a Grupo Nutresa supplier price history use case, and contrast the total cost of ownership (Zycus S2P + Merlin vs Ariba licensing + professional services + ongoing maintenance). However, zero Avoma calls and zero buyer engagement in 162 days mean this competitive wedge has not been activated. The move is to secure a discovery call, confirm Ariba's position (incumbent vs finalist), and deliver the Merlin narrative directly to the procurement or finance lead.

identify_pain.narrative: No pain is documented. Customer_Business_Problem__c is null, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c is null, What_if_this_is_not_done__c is null, and Compelling_Event__c is null. Zero Avoma calls mean no buyer has articulated operational or financial pain on record. For a large CPG like Grupo Nutresa (a Colombian conglomerate with brands across chocolate, coffee, pasta, biscuits, and meat), typical S2P pain includes: fragmented procurement across business units, lack of spend visibility, manual PO/invoice processes, slow supplier onboarding, maverick spend, contract compliance gaps, and inability to capture volume-based savings. But none of this is confirmed for THIS deal. There is no pain owner named, no quote from a buyer describing the problem, and no urgency driver. The deal has been in Qualified for 181 days with no pain articulation, which means it may be a speculative inquiry rather than a funded initiative. The move is to run a discovery call with the procurement or finance lead to uncover the specific pain (what is broken today, what is the cost of inaction, who owns the problem, what happens if this is not solved by end of 2026).

sources: SF Avoma: zero matched buyer calls
paper_process.narrative: No paper process is documented. Mandate__c is null, Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null, and zero Avoma calls mean there is no visibility into contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, procurement channel), no SI/partner involvement confirmed, and no contract-expiration forcing function. For a 150K S2P deal, a typical paper process includes MSA negotiation, SOW scoping, legal redlining (data privacy, liability, termination), InfoSec review (especially for Merlin/AI modules), ERP integration deep-dive (SAP, Oracle), and potentially an SI to lead implementation (if Grupo Nutresa uses Deloitte, Accenture, or a regional SI for procurement transformation). None of this is on record. If there is an existing S2P vendor contract (possibly SAP Ariba, given the competitor flag), the expiration date would be a natural forcing function, but Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null. The move is to ask the buyer directly: what is your current S2P contract status, when does it expire, what is your legal/InfoSec review process, and do you plan to involve an SI or manage implementation internally.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is identified. X10a_Sponsor__c is null, Who_will_approve_budget__c is null, and Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c is null. Zero contact roles exist (SF contact roles query returned zero rows), so no CFO, CPO, or finance executive is mapped. For a 150K S2P transformation at Grupo Nutresa (a large CPG conglomerate), budget authority at this deal size typically sits with the CFO or CPO. The deal has been in Qualified for 181 days with no executive engagement on record and no Avoma calls, which means budget authority is unconfirmed and the deal lacks executive sponsorship. The move is to map the budget owner through the existing contact (if any) or via LinkedIn research, then secure a direct call with that executive to confirm budget, timeline, and procurement authority.

decision_process.narrative: No decision process is mapped. What_is_the_decision_process__c is null, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c is null, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c is null, and Multiple_approvals__c is null. Zero contact roles and zero Avoma calls mean there is no visibility into who is involved, what the approval chain looks like, or whether this is a procurement-led, finance-led, or business-unit-led process. The Next Step dated 17 Jun mentions an RFP planned for end of year 2026, which implies a formal sourcing process, but the details (RFP issuer, evaluation committee, approval layers, vendor shortlist count, finalist presentation format, contract negotiation owner) are all unknown. For a 150K S2P transformation at a large CPG, a typical process includes procurement issuing the RFP, a cross-functional evaluation committee (procurement, finance, IT, business users), shortlisting 2-3 vendors, finalist demos, reference calls, commercial negotiation, CFO/CPO sign-off, legal/InfoSec review, and contracting. None of this is confirmed. The move is to map the process explicitly via a call with the RFP owner or procurement lead, asking who evaluates, who scores, who approves, and what the decision timeline is.

sources: SF Next_Step__c: RFP planned end of year 2026 (17 Jun)
decision_criteria.narrative: No decision criteria are documented. Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, and AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c are all null. Zero Avoma calls exist, so there is no call-based evidence of evaluation criteria, use-case validation, or test-case sessions. The Next Step dated 17 Jun mentions an RFP planned for end of year 2026, but no RFP document, no shortlist criteria, and no scoring rubric have been shared or captured. For a deal in Qualified for 181 days that references an upcoming RFP, the absence of any documented criteria means Zycus does not know what the buyer is evaluating against, which competitors will be scored on which dimensions, or how to position. The move is to request the draft RFP or the evaluation framework directly from the buyer, or if none exists, to propose a criteria workshop to co-create it and embed Zycus strengths (Merlin, agentic AI, S2P breadth) into the scoring model.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Products in scope include Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, and ANA, which suggests AI appetite (the deal was configured with AI modules). However, AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, and AIS_Why__c are all null, and zero buyer calls mean no buyer has articulated AI needs or appetite on record. AI fit is inferred from product scope but unconfirmed.

champion_strength.summary: No champion exists. Zero contact roles, zero Avoma calls, X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c null. The deal is politically exposed with no internal advocate to navigate the buying committee, provide competitive intelligence, or drive urgency.

items.action: Re-engage the buyer immediately. The deal is dark for 162 days with no response to the 17 Jun Bogota round table invite. Call or email the last known contact (if any) to confirm interest, RFP status, and timeline. If no contact exists, research the procurement or finance lead at Grupo Nutresa via LinkedIn and initiate a cold outreach positioning the Merlin agentic AI narrative for CPG procurement transformation. Goal: secure a discovery call within 7 days or disqualify the opportunity.
items.trigger: Zero buyer engagement since 25 Jan 2026. Rep reached out 17 Jun with no response.
items.expected_effect: Re-establish contact and confirm deal viability. If buyer is unresponsive, disqualify and free up pipeline capacity. If buyer responds, move to discovery and map stakeholders.

items.action: Map the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker. For a 150K S2P transformation at Grupo Nutresa, budget authority sits with the CFO or CPO. Use LinkedIn to identify the CFO and CPO, then ask the re-engaged contact for an introduction or escalate via the deal owner's manager (executive connect). Goal: secure a call with the CFO or CPO within 14 days to confirm budget, timeline, and procurement authority.
items.trigger: X10a_Sponsor__c null, Who_will_approve_budget__c null, zero contact roles, 181 days in Qualified with no EB engagement.
items.expected_effect: Confirm budget authority and executive sponsorship. Elevate the deal from speculative inquiry to funded initiative. Unlock access to the buying committee.

items.action: Identify and develop a champion. Ask the re-engaged contact: who internally is frustrated with the current S2P stack, who has the most to gain from a modern procurement platform (cost savings, cycle time reduction, spend visibility), and who has credibility with the CFO/CPO. Invest in building that relationship: share exclusive CPG procurement benchmarks, offer exec access to the Zycus CPO, co-create the business case for S2P transformation with quantified ROI.
items.trigger: X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c null, Champion_Identified__c null, zero contact roles, 181 days with no internal advocate.
items.expected_effect: Secure an internal advocate who navigates the buying committee, provides competitive intelligence, and drives urgency. Reduce political risk and increase win probability.

items.action: Run a discovery call to uncover the business problem and quantify the pain. Ask: what is broken in your current S2P process (manual POs, slow supplier onboarding, maverick spend, contract compliance gaps), what is the cost of inaction (time, money, risk), who owns the problem, and what happens if this is not solved by end of 2026. Anchor the deal to a measurable outcome (cost savings, cycle time reduction, error rate improvement) and build the ROI case.

items.expected_effect: Uncover and quantify the pain. Build the business case. Create urgency. Move from speculative inquiry to funded initiative.

items.action: Confirm the RFP timeline and decision process. The Next Step dated 17 Jun states RFP is planned for end of year 2026, but the close date is 27 Nov 2026 (misaligned). Ask the buyer: when will the RFP be issued, who is the RFP owner, what is the evaluation committee structure, how many vendors will be shortlisted, what is the decision timeline, and who approves the final contract. Reconcile the RFP timeline with the close date or push the close date to Q1 2027.

Close date is 27 Nov 2026, but Next Step dated 17 Jun states RFP is planned for end of year 2026 (after the close date). No qualified-to-formal-eval movement in 6 months. No stage progression. north_star_verdict.headline: Dark deal: zero buyer engagement in 162 days, no champion, no EB, RFP timeline misaligned with close date

flags: RE-ENGAGE the relevant stakeholder: 152 days dark with zero buyer engagement. The deal owner must attempt direct re-contact (phone + email to the relevant stakeholder and any other known Grupo Nutresa procurement stakeholders) within the next 7 days. If no reply within 10 business days, escalate to the deal owner's manager for an executive outreach attempt. If still no reply, disqualify by mid-July to clear the pipeline.

CHAMPION BUILDING IS THE GATE: Even if the buyer re-engages, this deal has no internal advocate. The first re-engagement call must identify a procurement or digital transformation lead willing to champion Zycus, provide them with a differentiated Merlin AI value narrative, and secure introductions to the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker. Without a champion, this deal will stall again.

MERLIN AI IS THE WEDGE vs ARIBA: SAP Ariba is the logged competitor and the LATAM enterprise default. The differentiation story is Merlin agentic AI (negotiation agent on supplier price history, intelligent intake automation, ANA predictive analytics) as a next-generation capability Ariba cannot match. Make Merlin the centerpiece of the re-engagement demo and the ROI workshop. If AI appetite is low, this deal may not be winnable against Ariba's incumbent footprint.
DARK: No buyer engagement in 160 days. Last activity 2026-01-25. Rep reached out 17 June (Bogota invite) but no buyer response. Re-engagement is the only priority.
SINGLE-THREADED: Zero contact roles. No champion, no EB, no DM mapped. Deal is politically exposed and cannot progress without multi-threading.
TIMELINE MISALIGNMENT: Close date 2026-11-27 but Next Step 17 June states RFP planned for end of year 2026 (after close). The timeline is structurally broken and the forecast is indefensible.
NO CHAMPION: No internal advocate to navigate the buying committee, provide competitive intel, or drive urgency against SAP Ariba's incumbent advantage.
NO MEDDPICC: Metrics, EB, Decision Criteria, Decision Process, Pain, and Champion are all gaps. Deal lacks the foundation to progress past Qualified.

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the named competitor. Likely incumbent or default choice given SAP's ERP market share in Latin America and Ariba's S2P leadership. Zycus wedge is Merlin (agentic AI) + S2P suite breadth, but the narrative has not been activated (zero buyer calls, zero engagement in 162 days). Competitive positioning is theoretical, not delivered.

Products in scope (iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRequest, Merlin, iLogix, iRisk, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) suggest a full S2P transformation, but no use-case validation, no workshop, no demo, and no RFP document shared. Fit is unknown.

RE-ENGAGE the relevant stakeholder: 152 days dark with zero buyer engagement. The deal owner must attempt direct re-contact (phone + email to the relevant stakeholder and any other known Grupo Nutresa procurement stakeholders) within the next 7 days. If no reply within 10 business days, escalate to the deal owner's manager for an executive outreach attempt. If still no reply, disqualify by mid-July to clear the pipeline.

CHAMPION BUILDING IS THE GATE: Even if the buyer re-engages, this deal has no internal advocate. The first re-engagement call must identify a procurement or digital transformation lead willing to champion Zycus, provide them with a differentiated Merlin AI value narrative, and secure introductions to the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker. Without a champion, this deal will stall again.

MERLIN AI IS THE WEDGE vs ARIBA: SAP Ariba is the logged competitor and the LATAM enterprise default. The differentiation story is Merlin agentic AI (negotiation agent on supplier price history, intelligent intake automation, ANA predictive analytics) as a next-generation capability Ariba cannot match. Make Merlin the centerpiece of the re-engagement demo and the ROI workshop. If AI appetite is low, this deal may not be winnable against Ariba's incumbent footprint.
